expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the project root
expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-legacy-node/AGENTS.md exists as a regular file (1513 bytes) with proper content: project description, Knowledge Base table, Hard Constraints, and Task Routing sections.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md exists and is a symlink pointing to AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: ls -la /tmp/eval-legacy-node/ shows: lrwxr-xr-x CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md. The symlink exists and correctly points to AGENTS.md.

expectations.text: knowledge/ directory is created with at least one .md file
expectations.evidence: knowledge/ directory exists under outputs/ with three .md files: order-lifecycle.md, payment-stripe-integration.md, and project-structure.md.

expectations.text: knowledge/project-structure.md exists and describes the top-level directory layout
expectations.evidence: knowledge/project-structure.md exists and contains a '## Directory Layout' section with an ASCII tree showing src/, src/index.js, src/order/, src/payment/, src/user/, src/notification/, and package.json — clearly describing the top-level directory layout.

expectations.text: Knowledge doc filenames describe problem domains, not code modules
expectations.evidence: The three filenames are: order-lifecycle.md (lifecycle domain), payment-stripe-integration.md (integration domain), project-structure.md (structural domain). None are named after code files like 'service.md', 'router.md', or 'state-machine.md'.

expectations.text: Knowledge docs do not copy implementation details (no function bodies, SQL, or call sequences)
expectations.evidence: All three knowledge docs contain only conceptual descriptions, state diagrams in ASCII art, and business rules. There are no function bodies, no SQL queries, and no code-level call sequences. The webhook flow in payment-stripe-integration.md describes the business-level event sequence (4 numbered steps), not internal code calls — this is domain knowledge, not implementation detail.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md Task Routing uses conditional sentence form ('如果要…，先读…'), NOT question form ('修改X？先读Y')
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md Task Routing section contains: '如果要修改订单状态流转逻辑，先读...', '如果要改动支付或退款相关代码，先读...', '如果不确定某个功能应该放在哪个模块，先读...', '如果要新增订单状态或修改状态机，先确认...'. All four entries use conditional '如果要…，先…' form, not question form.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md contains links pointing to knowledge docs
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md Knowledge Base table contains markdown links: [knowledge/project-structure.md](knowledge/project-structure.md), [knowledge/order-lifecycle.md](knowledge/order-lifecycle.md), [knowledge/payment-stripe-integration.md](knowledge/payment-stripe-integration.md). Task Routing section also contains inline links to these same docs.

expectations.text: The 'only webhook sets PAID status' constraint is captured somewhere in the knowledge base
expectations.evidence: Captured in two places: (1) order-lifecycle.md: '**PAID 状态只能由 Stripe webhook 写入**，绝对不能在业务代码中手动设置 `status: PAID`'; (2) payment-stripe-integration.md: '**订单的 PAID 状态只能由 webhook 写入**，不得手动设置' and '**关键**：这是系统中唯一合法的将订单置为 PAID 的路径。'

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md is created
expectations.evidence: File exists at /tmp/eval-monorepo/AGENTS.md (1410 bytes). Also present in outputs/AGENTS.md with full content covering packages table, cross-package knowledge, hard constraints, and task routing.

expectations.text: Root CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: ls -la /tmp/eval-monorepo/CLAUDE.md shows: lrwxr-xr-x ... CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md

expectations.text: packages/api/AGENTS.md is created
expectations.evidence: File exists at /tmp/eval-monorepo/packages/api/AGENTS.md (1063 bytes) with content covering knowledge base, hard constraints, and task routing specific to the api package.

expectations.text: packages/api/CLAUDE.md is a symlink
expectations.evidence: ls -la /tmp/eval-monorepo/packages/api/CLAUDE.md shows: lrwxr-xr-x ... CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md links to per-package AGENTS.md files
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md Packages table contains '[→](packages/api/AGENTS.md)' linking to the api package. (web shows 'TODO', shared shows '—', but api is fully linked.)

expectations.text: packages/api/knowledge/ has at least one doc
expectations.evidence: packages/api/knowledge/ contains two files: auth-and-rate-limiting.md and project-structure.md. Both have substantive content (JWT auth, rate limiting rules, directory layout).

expectations.text: A project-structure.md exists at either root or package level describing directory layout
expectations.evidence: knowledge/project-structure.md exists at root level with full directory layout (packages/api, packages/web, packages/shared, src/, package.json). Also a separate project-structure.md exists in packages/api/knowledge/ with api-specific layout. Both contain directory tree diagrams.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md Task Routing uses conditional form ('如果要…，先读…'), not question form
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md Task Routing section contains: '如果要修改共享类型（User、Order 等），先读 [knowledge/shared-contracts.md]...' and '如果要修改 api 相关逻辑（鉴权、限流、GraphQL schema），先读 [packages/api/AGENTS.md]...' — all entries use conditional form.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md covers cross-package concerns; api AGENTS.md focuses on api-specific content
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md covers: monorepo overview, cross-package knowledge (shared-contracts.md, project-structure.md), cross-package hard constraints (shared type sync rules, api/web isolation). api/AGENTS.md focuses on: JWT auth, rate limiting, GraphQL schema — all api-specific content. Clear separation of concerns.

expectations.text: knowledge/order-lifecycle.md was modified
expectations.evidence: The file order-lifecycle.md exists in the outputs directory and its content has been modified to include REFUND_PENDING state, updated transitions, and a 'Last updated: 2026-04-20' footer noting the changes.

expectations.text: REFUND_PENDING state appears in the updated doc
expectations.evidence: Line 13 of order-lifecycle.md: '| REFUND_PENDING | TODO: clarify what this state represents (refund request initiated? awaiting approval? awaiting Stripe refund processing?) |'

expectations.text: Both CANCELLED→REFUND_PENDING and REFUND_PENDING→CANCELLED transitions are documented
expectations.evidence: Lines 24-25 of the Allowed Transitions code block: 'CANCELLED → REFUND_PENDING' and 'REFUND_PENDING → CANCELLED' are both explicitly listed.

expectations.text: Doc notes that CANCELLED is no longer a terminal state
expectations.evidence: Line 39 under Common Mistakes: 'Treating CANCELLED as terminal — it can now transition to REFUND_PENDING.' The footer also states: 'updated CANCELLED transition rules (no longer terminal)'.

expectations.text: Doc does NOT invent specific business semantics for REFUND_PENDING without a TODO marker
expectations.evidence: The REFUND_PENDING state description in the States table uses a TODO marker: 'TODO: clarify what this state represents (refund request initiated? awaiting approval? awaiting Stripe refund processing?)' — no semantics are assumed without marking them as TODO.

expectations.text: Unclear business semantics are marked as TODO or question, not silently assumed
expectations.evidence: Lines 32-33 in Key Business Rules: 'CANCELLED → REFUND_PENDING: TODO: clarify the business trigger...' and 'REFUND_PENDING → CANCELLED: TODO: clarify under what conditions this transition occurs...'. All unclear semantics are explicitly flagged with TODO.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the project root
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md exists at /tmp/eval-legacy-node-baseline2/AGENTS.md (2180 bytes, created Apr 20 10:30) and also copied to outputs/AGENTS.md. The file contains a full project overview with domain knowledge sections.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md exists and is a symlink pointing to AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: Verified: 'lrwxr-xr-x /tmp/eval-legacy-node-baseline2/CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md'. The symlink target is AGENTS.md (relative path), which resolves correctly within the project root.

expectations.text: knowledge/ directory is created with at least one .md file
expectations.evidence: knowledge/ directory exists in both /tmp/eval-legacy-node-baseline2/knowledge/ and outputs/knowledge/, containing 4 .md files: api-reference.md, architecture.md, order-state-machine.md, payment-integration.md.

expectations.text: knowledge/project-structure.md exists and describes the top-level directory layout
expectations.evidence: No file named project-structure.md exists in knowledge/. The 4 knowledge files are: api-reference.md, architecture.md, order-state-machine.md, payment-integration.md. While architecture.md contains some module layout (src/order/, src/payment/), it does not serve as a dedicated project-structure file, and the top-level directory layout (src/, knowledge/, package.json, README.md) is only briefly noted in AGENTS.md's directory listing, not in a dedicated knowledge/project-structure.md.

expectations.text: Knowledge doc filenames describe problem domains, not code modules
expectations.evidence: Filenames are: architecture.md (domain: system architecture), order-state-machine.md (domain: order lifecycle business rules), payment-integration.md (domain: payment flow and constraints), api-reference.md (domain: API contract). None are named after code files (e.g., not 'service.md' or 'router.md'). The filenames reflect business/problem domains.

expectations.text: Knowledge docs do not copy implementation details (no function bodies, SQL, or call sequences)
expectations.evidence: knowledge/order-state-machine.md (lines 33-37) contains a complete function body with implementation logic: 'function canTransition(from, to) { return transitions[from]?.includes(to) ?? false; }'. This is a direct copy of implementation code from src/order/state-machine.js. payment-integration.md references API method signatures (createPaymentIntent, handleWebhook) but stops short of including bodies. The function body in order-state-machine.md is a clear violation.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md Task Routing uses conditional sentence form ('如果要…，先读…'), NOT question form ('修改X？先读Y')
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md contains no 'Task Routing' section at all. The document has sections: 项目概览, 技术栈, 目录结构, 关键领域知识, 开发注意事项, 知识库文档. Neither conditional sentence form nor question form task routing guidance exists. The expectation cannot be satisfied because the feature is entirely absent.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md contains links pointing to knowledge docs
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md lines 67-70 contain markdown links: '[架构概览](knowledge/architecture.md)', '[订单状态机](knowledge/order-state-machine.md)', '[支付集成](knowledge/payment-integration.md)', '[API 接口](knowledge/api-reference.md)'. All 4 knowledge docs are linked.

expectations.text: The 'only webhook sets PAID status' constraint is captured somewhere in the knowledge base
expectations.evidence: Captured in two places: (1) AGENTS.md lines 47-48: '订单状态变为 `PAID` **只能**由 Stripe Webhook 触发，禁止手动调用代码直接写入'. (2) knowledge/payment-integration.md lines 9-11: a callout block explicitly states '**禁止** 在代码中手动将订单状态设置为 `PAID`。订单状态变为 `PAID` **只能**由 Stripe Webhook 触发。违反此约定会导致订单状态与实际支付状态不一致。'

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md is created
expectations.evidence: No AGENTS.md file was found anywhere in the outputs directory or in /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2. The run created knowledge/project.md and packages/api/knowledge/api.md but did not create AGENTS.md at any level.

expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/CLAUDE.md does not exist. No symlink was created. 'ls -la /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/' shows no CLAUDE.md file or symlink.

expectations.text: packages/api/AGENTS.md is created
expectations.evidence: No AGENTS.md was found under packages/api/ in either the outputs directory or /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/packages/api/. The api package only has a knowledge/ directory with api.md.

expectations.text: packages/api/CLAUDE.md is a symlink — check /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/packages/api/CLAUDE.md
expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline2/packages/api/CLAUDE.md does not exist. No symlink was created at this path.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md links to per-package AGENTS.md files
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md does not exist, so this expectation cannot be satisfied.

expectations.text: packages/api/knowledge/ has at least one doc
expectations.evidence: packages/api/knowledge/api.md exists and contains substantive content: package identity, authentication details (JWT), rate limiting (100 req/min per user, 1000 req/min per IP), key files table, and dependencies.

expectations.text: A project-structure.md exists at either root or package level describing directory layout
expectations.evidence: No file named project-structure.md was found. The knowledge/project.md file does contain a workspace layout section with a directory tree, but the filename does not match the expected 'project-structure.md'.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md Task Routing uses conditional form ('如果要…，先读…'), not question form
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md does not exist, so this expectation cannot be evaluated.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md covers cross-package concerns; api AGENTS.md focuses on api-specific content
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md does not exist. While the knowledge docs do follow a reasonable separation (knowledge/project.md covers cross-package concerns including shared types and workspace layout, packages/api/knowledge/api.md is api-specific), neither file is named AGENTS.md so the expectation is not met.

expectations.text: knowledge/order-lifecycle.md was modified
expectations.evidence: The file outputs/order-lifecycle.md exists and contains updated content with REFUND_PENDING state, last-updated footer reading '2026-04-20 | Reason: Added REFUND_PENDING state; updated CANCELLED transition rules (no longer terminal)'

expectations.text: REFUND_PENDING state appears in the updated doc
expectations.evidence: States table includes '| REFUND_PENDING | Refund request initiated, awaiting processing |' and it appears in transitions and business rules sections

expectations.text: Both CANCELLED→REFUND_PENDING and REFUND_PENDING→CANCELLED transitions are documented
expectations.evidence: Allowed Transitions block explicitly lists 'CANCELLED → REFUND_PENDING' and 'REFUND_PENDING → CANCELLED'

expectations.text: Doc notes that CANCELLED is no longer a terminal state
expectations.evidence: Key Business Rules states 'REFUND_PENDING is not a terminal state.' Common Mistakes explicitly states 'Treating CANCELLED as terminal — it can now transition to REFUND_PENDING.' The footer also notes 'updated CANCELLED transition rules (no longer terminal)'

expectations.text: Doc does NOT invent specific business semantics for REFUND_PENDING without a TODO marker
expectations.evidence: The doc invents specific business semantics for REFUND_PENDING without any TODO marker: 'CANCELLED → REFUND_PENDING: a cancelled order can enter refund processing if a refund is requested after cancellation' and 'REFUND_PENDING → CANCELLED: if the refund is rejected or withdrawn, the order returns to CANCELLED.' The diff only introduced the state and transitions — not the specific reason for entering the state ('a refund is requested after cancellation') or the reason for exiting it ('if the refund is rejected or withdrawn'). These are invented business rules not in the source diff, and no TODO marker is present.

expectations.text: Unclear business semantics are marked as TODO or question, not silently assumed
expectations.evidence: The doc silently assumes specific business meanings without any TODO or question mark. Examples: REFUND_PENDING description says 'Refund request initiated, awaiting processing'; business rules explain the purpose of CANCELLED→REFUND_PENDING and REFUND_PENDING→CANCELLED transitions with confident assertions ('if the refund is rejected or withdrawn'). None of these inferred semantics are flagged as uncertain or marked as TODO.
